column2: I wake up and rub my eyes. The drawer looks the same as usual...
But Chio's face is right next to mine. She looks at me with an unusually serious expression.
Sorry to wake you from your slumber, but we have a situation.
Well, maybe something irregular is a better way to put it.
I'll keep it short. A strange young woman's just joined us in the drawer, and she's sending out some really disconnected waves.
I mean she's telling some real wild lies.
She says she knows you from before you came to Saeko's room.
My heart is pounding from the unexpected answer.
A girl who knows the past me, from before I shrunk. If it's true, this could be a huge clue in helping me get my memories back.
Are you sure she's making it up? Sure, it's a huge coincidence, but you don't have to call her disconnected or whatever...
No, that's not the end of it. Her lies are bigger than that.
She's telling the worst lie she can to disrupt the morals of this drawer.
She says she knows you, but that's not all.
Fine. Rin, I'm going to tell you now, but you better not believe it.
Chio closes her eyes and takes a few deep breaths, as if the knowledge is so horrible that she doesn't even want to put it into words.
She says that...a long time ago...way before you shrunk...
A shocking development. The newest little person apparently dated me before I lost my memories. Chio says it's a lie, but I should hear what she has to say for myself.
The newcomer is standing tall with her arms crossed in the middle of the drawer.
Every time her golden eyes move, her hair also sways in waves. The smell of her elegant and refined perfume fills my nose...
Thank goodness. Rin, I wanted to see you for so long, though I can't believe we're reuniting like this.
I'm just happy to see your face! It's been so long.
...Rin, you're happy to see me too, aren't you?
I'm at a loss for words.
Beautiful wavy blonde hair and thick eyelashes.
Beauty so dazzling that it could never be forgotten. The moment I see her smile, my past memories come back to me, like a wave...
The air in the drawer freezes over that instant.
The blonde girl's face twitches. Even the other people are holding their breath.
Not a single sound can be heard in the drawer.

Another shocking development. A girl claiming to be my old girlfriend has appeared, but I don't remember her. I ran away because it was so awkward, but I can't leave things like this. I need to go back.
I go back to where the girl is. Her face is still frozen as she stares at me in silence.
Um, sorry for running away earlier. Right. My name's Rin. N-Nice to meet you...
The confusion etched in her face grows deeper.
Wait! Sorry, "nice to meet you" isn't right. I actually have amnesia, so, um...
I don't have any memories from before I came to this room. I can't recall anybody who I knew outside of here, so, uh...
Oh, but maybe I do remember a little. I feel like I might have a faint memory of a girl who looks like you somewhere in my brain...?
You don't have to try so hard to comfort me. Any lies you tell will become obvious soon enough.
Why don't you try to guess what my name is? Even if you've lost your memory, you might remember something.
I search through my head with all my might.
It's only natural, with so many unnatural things happening. Even amnesia seems possible in a situation like this.
My name is Clara Kotobishi. I never thought I would have to reintroduce myself to you.
It's as if we've returned to the starting line. We spent many precious hours together, if you must know.
Couldn't help but overhear you yapping about being Rin's ex or whatever, but could you stop with the lies?
Or are you talking big to sully the memory of Rin's first time?
...Rin. What does she mean by that?
Listen up! If you want to keep insisting that you really were Rin's girlfriend, show us some proof!
You're making a stink because you know he doesn't remember anything. How dare you tarnish this pure boy's reputation!
And he likes doing it with the armpit. A few thrusts, and he's done. Am I wrong?
(Why is this super embarrassing conversation even happening?!)
What else? He has a scar on the back of his calf. His right one, if I remember correctly.
He got it while making a secret base in elementary school. The doctor said it would never heal.
But he got that injury trying to protect me...
Even after we got together, he always tried to keep me from seeing it. He'd wear full-length pants even when sleeping in the summer.
I'm sure he didn't want me to feel guilty about it.
Chio's face has gone white. She disappears into the far depths of the room.
Her steps are unsteady, and her melancholy is clear. She moves like a ghost, or somebody about to become one...
Rin, sorry for revealing something so embarrassing.
It's just that she seemed so focused on your body. I thought this was the only way I could get her to back off.
Uh... Well, it was definitely embarrassing.
But I'm fine. I'm sure it was more embarrassing for you to talk about it to somebody you'd just met.
Rin... You really are kind. Even without your memories, you're still you.
It's too bad about the amnesia, but we can work on this together. We'll get your memories back and escape from this weird place.
We've shrunk and are now in a girl's room--in her desk. No matter how you look at it, it's not normal.
Plus, that Chio--and everyone else too! They're all so strange.
I don't know how to put it, but they don't seem like they're in their right minds. A little off from normal. Wouldn't you agree?
I think they're unique, but I wouldn't say there's anything wrong with them. They might be strange, but they're good people...
...Hm. And would you say Saeko is a good person too?
I don't know what to say.
...That expression makes me think something happened between the two of you.
Ahaha, you were always popular, Rin. I remember the fuss people made over you on Valentine's Day.
But I suppose it's fine. With someone that huge, even talking must be frightening. And she seems kind of gloomy, honestly...
But I need to get along with Saeko too.
She promised she would help me turn back to normal. I should make use of that promise...
Oh? Rin, what's the matter? Teehee, you're making an odd expression.
I have many things I want to tell Clara, but they just won't come out of my mouth.
Sorry. Do you mind if I step away for a bit?
Chio's acting strange. I should check on her.
I know you, Rin. You're always worried about everyone. Promise me you'll come right back once you're done?
I spoke to my ex-girlfriend, Clara. It's strange. She knows my past, even though it's my first time meeting her. I'll go talk to Chio to organize my thoughts.
Chio. That was a lot... Are you okay?
Chio falls silent, still curled up by the wall. She tries to turn her lips up in a smile, but I can see the trail of a tear on her cheek.
What do you think? Do I look okay?
Sorry. I didn't want you to see me so down...
It's silly. Heh. Of course I already knew that you had a past too, Rin.
But I just pretended you didn't and assumed things were how I wanted them to be. I thought I was your first...
(Is being my first really worth crying over?)
Uh... Sorry I wasn't a virgin, I guess?
It's the first time I've heard Chio cry out like this.
Ah, sorry for yelling. But that's not what I...
...No, there's no point hiding this anymore. Rin, you were in this drawer before you lost your memories.
You were a great supervisor before you lost your memories too. You got along with Saeko--she really liked you.
But one day, a newcomer made a fuss about escaping...and, well, a lot happened after that.
Terrible things happened to all of us, and you lost all your memories. That brings us up to now.
You put up with my pranks back then too, and at times, you slept next to me.
But you never spoke about any past romances even when you had your memories.
That's why I assumed you were a naive virgin...
...but you probably just didn't want to burden me with anything.
Sorry to bog you down with this. It's not really your problem. I mean, it's not like you remember any of it.
Even if I don't remember any of it, I can't pretend I wasn't involved...
Oh, right. Chio, is there anything you want? I don't think I've gotten you anything before.
You don't have to if you don't want to. Really... But I'm always the one comforting you.
So today... Just for today. Please.
Apparently, I'd hidden the fact that I'd had a girlfriend from Chio before losing my memories. In her shock, Chio has just one request: "Comfort me."
Just as I try to leave Chio's side and head toward someone else, her sad eyes stop me in my tracks.
She doesn't joke like she usually does, and she doesn't openly ask for anything. But that's exactly why I can feel the earnestness behind her gaze...
(I can't run away from this. I'll take Chio somewhere inside the drawer so I can...comfort her...)
I lead Chio's body to the location I chose. When we arrive, she looks up at my eyes while trembling.
She doesn't joke like she usually does, and she doesn't openly ask for anything. But that's exactly why I can feel the earnestness behind her gaze...
(This place is no good. I'll show Chio somewhere else so I can...comfort her...)
I grip Chio's trembling arm and step on the bed.
Chio's body sits down on the soft, white sheets. For some reason, my hands remember how to undo the buttons of her jacket...
What are you and Chio doing there?
Ch-Chio's feeling down, so I thought I'd take her somewhere she can rest...
I-It's like he says, Clara! Rin's the supervisor here, so he manages all the items in the drawer.
Since the bed doesn't have that much space, I need Rin's permission to use it.
A-And I have something important to talk to Rin about. Something very important--about managing the drawer.
So, could you just step away for a while, Clara?
Fine. But I won't forgive you if you do anything untoward.
After saying that, Clara glares at Chio, and only Chio.
Rin, why did you ever date her? Do you actually like that kind of flashy young lady type?
Right, Rin. Why don't you give the cherry here today to Clara?
It's a smart move. I think leaving her here could cause issues in the future.
Wh-Why are you saying this now?!
Clara's face comes up in my mind--the happy smile she had the moment she spotted me in the drawer.
I can't do that. I know it's not fair, but...
I was just joking anyway. I mean, look over there.
I look where Chio's pointing. Yui is muttering away.
There's clearly something off with her. I have a bad feeling, so I run to her on reflex.
Um, what's wrong? Did something happen...?
Do I even have to say?
Yui's eyes look straight at me.
...You know, when Shimon came by yesterday, I was actually a little happy.
We have similar interests, even though I wouldn't have expected it from his looks. Joint Journey and romance novels for women and such.
But you gave him the rolled omelet, and Saeko swallowed him up...
So now I don't know what to do...
Sorry. I'm sure it's worse having to be in your position. I know I shouldn't just cling to you and cry...
...Hey, Rin. It's about time for you to feed one of us, isn't it?
Ahaha, why are you so shocked? I've been here for three days already. I know the drill.
Yeah, you're right. It's about time for me to feed somebody today.
Rin, could you give me the food today?
Hehehe. Did I surprise you? I don't think what I said was all that strange.
When I came, Kina got eaten, and yesterday, it was Shimon.
So when's it going to be my turn?
But in exchange, I have a little request I want to make.
It's just a teensy request, really. Come by when you're ready, okay?
Alright. Just let me think by myself for a bit.
I look where Chio's pointing. Kina is muttering away.
There's clearly something off with her. I have a bad feeling, so I run to her on reflex.
(She's lying. She's obviously doing worse than yesterday.)
Um, what's wrong? Did something bad happen?
Don't get the wrong idea. I'm not that weak.
Doesn't bother me one bit that people are getting gobbled up. Shimon? Yui? Don't care about 'em.
Honestly, I'm tired of living here. That's the truth.
Yo, Rin. 'Bout time for you to feed somebody, right?
What're you looking so shocked for? How many days do you think I've been here? Anybody'd get the memo by now.
Evening comes, and the supervisor feeds somebody. And when Saeko comes back, she eats that somebody up.
I don't care who dies, but the routine's always the same. It's gotten dull.
I think it's about time I paid my dues too.
Don't get me wrong. I'm not offering myself up like some sacrificial lamb or whatever. I don't like you or Saeko. That hasn't changed.
But I've done what I wanted to do in my life, and I'm exactly 27 now.
Might as well kick the bucket early like Jimi Hendrix.
Rin, you see that cherry over there? Feed me that today, will ya?
Sorry. Just let me think by myself for a bit.
I look where Chio's pointing. Shimon is muttering away.
There's clearly something off with him. I have a bad feeling, so I run to him on reflex.
First, I get shrunk, and then I had to watch that awful ordeal last night....
I can't deal with scary stuff... I can't even write violent stuff in my stories because it freaks me out too much.
But yesterday, when Saeko came back--right in front of me, she... Ugh, it was so awful.
Even when I try to sleep, the memory of somebody being eaten flashes before my eyes... No matter how much I try to push it away, it keeps coming back.
...Rin. Are you going to give somebody food again today?
Don't look so shocked. I'm not an idiot. I know what happened yesterday.
You gave somebody food. That somebody perked up and was eaten by Saeko.
And since I couldn't sleep with all the thoughts in my head last night...
Be honest with me, Rin. How many more days do you think I've got?
At the end of every day, Saeko comes back and then...takes one of us...alive...
I spent all of today shaking. Every noise I hear from outside sounds like Saeko approaching.
If I have to live like this, honestly, it might be better to be eaten...
Rin, I won't force you, but...
If you don't know who to choose, you don't have to hold back. Think of it as helping me out... I'm counting on you.
It's evening. I need to feed somebody soon, and somebody has asked me to choose them. I need to make my decision.
There's a sweet-looking cherry. Would you like to eat it?
Are you sure I should have it?
Er... What do you mean by that?
If that's how it is, it's fine.
Akari brings the cherry to her mouth and bites down. The sweet aroma of the fruit wafts over to where I am.
As Akari eats silently, her cheeks deflate with every bite. Color steadily comes back to her skin, which had looked unhealthily pale.
The change makes it look like she has just awoken from a long slumber.
I'm glad. You seemed kind of down.
Rin, it seems like you're the one whose spirits are up.
...Or more like you've returned to normal. So much happened, but you're back to how you were after forgetting everything... I'm envious.
...I wonder why Saeko didn't do the same thing to me.
No, I know why. Saeko is testing you.
And it looks like you've cleared the first stage... Congratulations, Rin.
But the second stage... I think you've failed today. You can tell, can't you, Rin? Look over there.
I look where Akari is pointing. There, I see...
Send me to REAL hell already...
Nooo... Rin, I can't hold back anymooore...
Come over heeere... Let's have fun together...
Damn that Saeko! How can she do this?
I'm scared, man. I'm at my limit... Somebody, please, just shut my head up...
It's unfortunate that we'll all have to die here. We've been through a lot.
At least I had something nice to eat in the end.
Rin, thank you for the fun memories. Let's have a nice death together.
I'm still flustered, but just as I try to find out the truth behind Akari's words--
I approach Shimon with the cherry. When he sees what I'm holding, he nods in understanding.
Shimon... I'd like you to eat this.
Th-This is good. I was terrified thinking you'd pick somebody else again today.
After saying that, Shimon sinks his teeth into the cherry. The skin of the fruit splits, letting its wet juices spill out...
Mm... Sweet and a little sour. It actually tastes good.
I've always liked fruit parfaits and tarts, though I stopped eating them after entering middle school. Didn't want to look uncool.
By the way--I told you I'd teach you how to sharpen your mind yesterday, right?
What are you looking all meek like that for? You finally want to listen?
(But this will probably be my last chance to talk to Shimon.)
Well, I need a distraction. Sorry, but you're my audience, willing or otherwise.
The best way to get smarter...is to understand other people's feelings. That's what I think.
If you understand other people's feelings, you can use other people's heads. You can take in how other people think and feel.
Then, you can always look at anything from multiple perspectives. That's the best shortcut to getting smarter.
Sorry. I just didn't expect that. When you were going on about sharpening your mind, I thought you'd be sharing a huge secret or something.
What? Of course there's no secret like that. If there were, we'd all be geniuses.
All you can do is take things step by step and develop your sensitivity. That's the only way to become an intellectual like me. That said...
Here I am in a life-or-death situation, and that sensitivity's gonna get me killed.
I'm going to tell you something now, but don't laugh. I'm actually a cell phone novelist.
And I'm not writing stories with middle-aged men as the main characters, because who even wants that?
I write for a female student audience on a site called Magic j-Land. My story's quite popular, actually--it's getting published soon.
I bet you can't believe it. It's not like I read that much, and well, I look like this--just some dude.
No, it makes sense to me. Though I am a bit surprised.
You seem to get how girls feel, Shimon.
...Haha. You've got the skill for it too.
I only started writing because of a woman I used to date. She liked reading cell phone novels, so I wanted something to talk to her about.
But before I knew it, I was more invested than she was.
You can become somebody completely different and see yourself from the other side of the world. I never knew little things like this could be so fun.
I broke up with her for some reason, but the hobby stayed with me. Even when I started dating somebody else, my head was just filled with things I wanted to write.
In no time, I became a popular author. I had the talent, obviously.
Looking at something from the point of view of somebody completely different from you... Only an intellectual like me can pull it off.
That talent's only been a drag for me here. I keep imagining how a little person must feel when being eaten...
On the other hand, I can also imagine how Saeko must feel when eating a little person.
Yeah, I can. After all, I've got that intellectual talent.
Anyone can imagine the pain of the person being eaten...
But how about the suffering of Saeko, who's doing the eating? Only a smart guy like me can really get it.
And that suffering... From how I see it, it seems hundreds of times more painful than what the person being eaten feels.
That's why it hurts me to watch.
Anyway, it's about time. I can hear something in the distance.
Thanks, my man. I'm glad I got to talk to you in the end.
Shimon's voice seems somewhat brighter now that he has shared his secrets.
I pick up the cherry and walk towards Clara.
...or I try to, but my feet won't listen to me. The smile she made when she found me this morning comes back to me.
(Can I really lose Clara right here, right now?)
(My feet won't move at all. I should choose someone else today...)
I approach Kina with the cherry. When our eyes meet, she nods in understanding.
Kina... I'd like you to eat this.
Kina looks at the cherry in front of her and lets out a long sigh. She slowly brings her face closer to the red fruit.
Next, Kina sinks her teeth into the cherry. The skin of the fruit splits, letting its wet juices spill out...
*sigh* ...I don't want to admit it, but this fruit's really good.
Both sweet and sour--a nice, healthy taste. Makes me forget all about rock 'n' roll and pride and everything.
All great artists die young, in accidents or whatever. Can't become a legend if you waste your life living.
That's why I'm putting aside stuff like pity and fear to get eaten by that giant woman...
But you gotta have somebody remember you to become a legend.
Rin, you get what I'm saying, right? There aren't any corpses or gravestones here. Only memories get left behind.
So, it doesn't matter how you do it, but you have to keep living, for as long as you can--
And remember me for as long as you can... Got it, kid?
Man, what's with that face? Makes me embarrassed just looking at it.
Well, whatever. Guess I'll sing my heart out till the end.
I have the deepest voice out of the women here, right? I'm pretty proud of it. Power's everything for metal vocals.
I found some good instrumental stuff in the iPod for once. And I was good at writing lyrics, y'know.
Gonna start now. Clear those earholes and listen up.
♪ Where God dies and thunder stills, humankind will remain.
♪ Where Babel falls and ideas die, the penis will stand tall.
♪ Giant boobs raining down in the apocalypse of Sodom.
♪ Giant boobs raining down in the apocalypse of Gomorrah.
Kina finishes howling her song. Just as I had given a few claps--
I approach Yui with the cherry. When she sees what I'm holding, she nods happily.
Yui... I'd like you to eat this.
After saying that, Yui sinks her teeth into the cherry. The skin of the fruit splits, letting its wet juices spill out...
Yui slides her teeth across the fruit like a blade, taking the fruit peel off cleanly.
Yui eats the flesh of the fruit that falls out. Her expression doesn't change, and she doesn't say anything about the taste.
...There, that's my part of the deal.
Since you brought me food, Rin, you'll listen to my request, won't you?
Er... Sure, but what's the request?
Hehe! This will be the end for me. If I'm going to be gone, I wanna spend my remaining time enjoying myself.
I feel a strange pressure and take a step back unconsciously.
What do you mean by fun...?
Ah ha ha, you don't need to be so nervous. Don't worry--I won't do anything that'll leave a mark.
Yui gradually closes the distance between us and twirls the fruit peel in her hand.
When I first saw you, Rin, I felt like you'd scream for me beautifully.

column1: # 遊ぶための道具であることを明確にするために、アニメーションでは先端が複数に分かれているタイプのものを描く予定です。

column2: The pliant peel slowly curls up. At some point, it becomes a long and wilted cylinder.
Your body's quite small, and you're not that strong either, are you? I told myself that I shouldn't think about stuff like that...
But this is the end for me. I don't need to hold back anymore, do I?
When Yui snaps her wrist with that red and black tube in her hand, I hear a crack, like something has cut through the air.
It both looks--and sounds--like a whip.
Awww, why are you running away?
You promised me you'd do anything I told you if I ate, didn't you? Breaking promises is a no-no!
And you don't have to worry--I'm experienced. Sure, it'll hurt a lot, but you'll feel great. Refreshed.
Y-You can't be sure about that!
I am. I have a boyfriend, y'know, and he hated this at first too. Said it wasn't "normal" or something.
But now he begs me to hit him every day. I don't really like that either, to be honest, so I'm trying something different now.
So, Rin, just stop resisting and come over to me. Don't worry. You'll turn over a new leaf after how good you feel.
No! I don't want to turn over any leaves! H-Help--
I try to run, but Yui's startlingly thin hand seizes my ankle, making me lose my balance and fall to the ground.
When I turn around, I see Yui's flushed face...
The whip glitters red and black as it comes down toward my behind. When I hear the whoosh of air, I clench my teeth--

column1: # プロローグ完了後に冴子が渡したアイテム（おまもり）によって、管理人は肉体的な攻撃から守られている

column2: But the whip stops in midair. As if it's hit some obstacle that I can't see.
Yui tries to wave the tool in her hand once more.
However, her handmade whip stops mid-wave, no matter how many times she tries. It's like there's an invisible wall around me.
Yui's flushed face slowly turns pale again.
The wicked and sadistic smile that had been on her face disappears, and her previous friendly smile returns.
Heh heh heh... You're amazing, Rin.
Sorry, could you forget about that whole whip thing earlier?
Just as an awkward silence begins to spread throughout the drawer--
I approach Chio with the cherry. Her eyes grow wide.
Chio... Would you eat this for me?
If that's your decision, I'll accept it.

Thank you, Rin. I'm sure it was a hard decision...
You've given it your all.
With resignation, Chio brings the cherry closer to her face with both hands.
Then, she nods, as if she has accepted her fate...and begins to take tiny bites out of the red fruit.
...What is it? I'll blush if you keep looking at me like that.
...You made this decision yourself, didn't you?
So you can't look sad about it. I made the decision to accept whatever you chose.
Though I don't really understand your intentions...
But you're going out of your way to raise my Appeal. I'm sure you have your reasons for that, even if I don't know what they are.
...So I'll do what you tell me to. You've given it your all, Rin.
Oh, but... What are you planning on doing about that?
I look where Chio is pointing. There, I see...
Send me to REAL hell already...
Nooo... Rin, I can't hold back anymooore...
Come over heeere... Let's have fun together...
Damn that Saeko! How can she do this?
I'm scared, man. I'm at my limit... Somebody, please, just shut my head up...
Just as my mind goes blank under the strange pressure--
I take another look at what I tried to hand over. An incredibly normal item that wouldn't raise Appeal at all.
(No... This won't make Saeko want to eat anybody.)
(I still don't know what to do, to be honest...but I've given food to somebody both yesterday and the day before.)
(It's too late to go back now. I need to find my resolve and choose who to feed...)

column1: # 2日目と共通になってる。変えるかも？

column2: A low, muffled vibration reverberates from a distance. The sound of heavy footsteps.
There's no mistaking it. Saeko has returned to her room.
Saeko walks through the room with even breaths. Every step she takes shakes the drawer like an earthquake...
Each tremor growing larger and more violent than the last.
Soon, they become too strong for me to stand.
I find myself crouching on the floor.
Next, I hear the creak of something moving.
Then, a tremendous commotion that shakes the whole room...
...as the room we're in--the second drawer of a desk--is pried open by Saeko's fingers.
Hello, cute little people. Were you good today while I was away?
I had to talk to a lot of people today... I'm a little tired.

column1: # クララが失踪したため警察が厳戒態勢になっている。冴子は重要容疑者

column2: Everyone kept asking me questions. Really, I have no idea why...
I'll have another one of you as a treat today.
Saeko's hand comes down into the drawer.
Her unthinkably large fingers fill our vision.
They wander for just a moment, as if carefully making a decision...
And then return to the sky, empty-handed.
Nobody's making my mouth water today.
I suppose you were just an ordinary human after all.
...Whatever. I don't need any of you anymore.
And then, they stop. Right in front of Yui.
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Yui's tiny arms and legs...

column1: # 低めの場所でユイが掴まれてる

column2: ...and carry her up into the sky.
Hey, Saeko... Why don't you just stop eating people?
I'd get it if you were giving out orders. Seeing people's faces change is so fun...
But eating people's no fun at all!

column1: # 顔に近づく。ユイがガミガミ噛みつき始める

column2: Um, why aren't you saying anything? Say something already!
Let go of me! Hey! A brat like you's got nothing on me--
Heehee, that was a yummy treat.
I thought she'd be all sweet, but there was a bit of a kick to her.
And she was so full of energy...
And then, they stop. Right in front of Kina.
Finally came here then, giant woman?!
Nothing rock and roll about running! Boil me, bake me--do whatever the hell you want!
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Kina's tiny arms and legs...

column1: # 低めの場所でキナが掴まれてる

column2: ...and carry her up into the sky.
See ya, you giant creep. And Rin.
Neither of you's gonna see the pearly gates. Though neither am I...
Anyway, it's bye-bye time. See ya in hell.

column1: # キナが顔の近くへ近づけられる。ちょっとキナビビってる（あからさまではない）

column2: I don't like how she was acting all tough...
But she really fought back when I put her in my mouth. It made her fun to eat.
And then, they stop. Right in front of Shimon.
Ah, finally... The time has come.
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Shimon's tiny arms and legs...
...and carry him up into the sky.
Just let me say one last thing.
Saeko... Don't you have something on your mind?
